description:
The memory-first coding agent

Letta Code is a memory-first coding harness, designed for long-lived agents that can learn from experience.
Instead of working in independent sessions, you work with a persisted agent whose memory is portable across models (Claude, GPT, Gemini, GLM, Kimi, and more).
What's Changed
- fix: bump pi-ai for Fable local backend support by @overlord-letta in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2806
- docs: fix README feature links by @overlord-letta in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2807
- fix(listen): keep local channel listeners alive with process anchor by @shelleypham in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2804
- fix(local): inherit conversation model for subagents by @cpacker in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2796
- fix(local): avoid guessed endpoint model defaults by @cpacker in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2797
- fix: isolate background log files in private temp dirs by @devanshrj in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2812
- fix(local): align provider defaults with Pi TUI by @cpacker in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2811
- fix: pass Fable max reasoning to pi-ai by @overlord-letta in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2809
